Client 2: And I’m William. I’m more on the “make sure we’re protected” side—like, worst-case stuff.

Advisor: And cash—checking or savings you consider part of the plan?
Client 1: We’ve got 60700 at the bank, also sitting on the advisor platform.

Client 1: Yes, private markets—14900, also on the advisor platform.
Advisor: And the home—what are you valuing the primary residence at right now?

Advisor: Okay—thanks, Patricia and William. Two quick household items so I don’t make assumptions. First, what state are you residents of for planning and taxes?
Client 1: It’s listed as Other for our state of residence.
Advisor: Got it. And just so you know how I work—my advisory channel is RIA (registered investment advisor). Is that the relationship you’re expecting here, versus like a brokerage-only setup?
Client 2: Yes, RIA (registered investment advisor) is what we’re looking for.
Advisor: Perfect. Now, zooming out for a second—how would you describe your risk tolerance when markets get choppy? Conservative, moderate, aggressive—what fits best?

Advisor: Got it. And just so you know how I work—my advisory channel is RIA (registered investment advisor). Is that the relationship you’re expecting here, versus like a brokerage-only setup?
Client 2: Yes, RIA (registered investment advisor) is what we’re looking for.
Advisor: Perfect. Now, zooming out for a second—how would you describe your risk tolerance when markets get choppy? Conservative, moderate, aggressive—what fits best?
Client 1: Moderate. I’d say our risk tolerance is moderate.
Advisor: Okay. And when you say moderate, Patricia, is that like “we can ride out normal swings,” but you don’t want anything that threatens monthly cash flow?

Advisor: Hi Patricia, hi William—nice to meet you both. Before we get into goals, can you give me the quick version of what you want help with?
Client 1: Thanks. I’m Patricia—mostly I want a clear plan. I’m self-employed, income moves around, and I don’t want to mess up cash flow while still investing.
Client 2: And I’m William. I’m more on the “make sure we’re protected” side—like, worst-case stuff.
Advisor: Perfect—let’s start with cash flow. What’s the household gross income per year right now?
Client 1: Household gross is 101700 annually.

Client 1: Yes, that’s the long-term care policy.
Advisor: Thanks—super helpful. Before we wrap today, I just want to lock down a few household basics for the plan, Patricia and William.
Advisor: First one: marital status—are you two married, or partnered in another way?
Client 1: We’re married.
Advisor: Got it. Next—country for your planning assumptions. Are we planning everything as US-based?

Client 1: Yes—2 adults.
Advisor: Great, thank you. Last one is taxes. When we’re thinking about marginal rates, what tax bracket band should I assume you’re in right now?
Client 2: I think it’s 37%—yeah, 37%.
Advisor: Okay, that helps. And just to sanity-check—nothing about today’s numbers changes that assumption in the near term, it’s still the bracket you’re planning around?
Client 1: Right—still 37% is what we’re using.
